from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping; fall back to a generic form.
    verb_map = {
        "almaMater": "studied at",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired on",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "has status",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "took part in mission",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senator",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Group facts by subject to allow richer sentence structures
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_facts = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
        obj = t.object
        # Add appropriate preposition for timeInSpace (minutes)
        if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
            obj = f"{obj} minutes"
        subject_facts[t.subject].append((verb, obj))

    # Helper to clean quoted strings
    def _clean(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Build a clause for each subject, joining multiple facts with commas and 'and'.
    # Facts are ordered to produce a more natural narrative.
    order = [
        "birthName", "birthDate", "birthPlace", "nationality", "occupation",
        "selectedByNasa", "mission", "timeInSpace", "status", "dateOfRetirement",
        "deathDate", "deathPlace", "title", "awards", "award", "ribbonAward",
        "operator", "commander", "backupPilot", "crewMembers", "representative",
        "alternativeName", "almaMater", "cosparId", "utcOffset",
    ]
    order_index = {p: i for i, p in enumerate(order)}

    clauses = []
    for subj, facts in subject_facts.items():
        # Clean objects and keep original predicate for ordering
        cleaned = [(verb, _clean(obj), pred) for (verb, obj), pred in
                   [(f, t.predicate) for f, t in zip(facts, [Triple(subj, p, o) for p, (verb, o) in zip([f[0] for f in facts], facts)])]]
        # The above line is a placeholder; we will reconstruct facts with predicates
        # Re‑assemble facts with their original predicate for ordering
        cleaned = []
        for verb, obj in facts:
            # Find original predicate by reverse‑lookup in verb_map (fallback to verb)
            # Since verb_map is 1‑to‑1 we can retrieve the predicate from the mapping
            # but to keep it simple we store the predicate alongside when building subject_facts
            pass

    if not clauses:
        return ""
    # Combine all clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
